# Feature 6: Match Dashboard

## Overview

The match dashboard gives job seekers and employers a central place to view, sort, filter, and act on AI-generated match recommendations. Job seekers see matches ranked by score with the ability to accept or decline. Employers see matched candidates per job posting with candidate details. When both parties accept, the employer receives the job seeker's contact information. Email notifications keep users informed of new matches and status changes.

**Business Value:** Completes the core matching loop — matches generated by the AI (Feature 5) are now surfaced, actionable, and lead to real connections between seekers and employers.

---

## User Stories

### User Story 1: Job Seeker Views Matches

**As a** job seeker
**I want** to see all my matches ranked by confidence score
**So that** I can focus on the strongest opportunities first

**Acceptance Criteria:**

- [ ] Matches displayed in a list sorted by confidence score (strongest first) by default
- [ ] Each match card shows: confidence badge, match summary, employer status, seeker status, and match date
- [ ] User can switch sort order between "Best Match" (score) and "Newest" (date)
- [ ] Empty state shown when no matches exist with an explanation of how matching works

**Priority:** High

---

### User Story 2: Job Seeker Filters Matches

**As a** job seeker
**I want** to filter my matches by status
**So that** I can manage pending, accepted, and declined matches separately

**Acceptance Criteria:**

- [ ] Filter tabs or dropdown for: All, Pending, Accepted, Declined
- [ ] Counts displayed per filter category
- [ ] Active filter persists during the session
- [ ] Filter resets to "All" on page reload

**Priority:** High

---

### User Story 3: Job Seeker Accepts or Declines a Match

**As a** job seeker
**I want** to accept or decline a match recommendation
**So that** I can control which employers receive my contact information

**Acceptance Criteria:**

- [ ] Accept and Decline buttons visible only when the seeker's status is Pending
- [ ] Confirmation prompt shown before declining (accept does not require confirmation)
- [ ] After action, the match status updates immediately in the UI without full page reload
- [ ] Once accepted or declined, the action cannot be reversed
- [ ] Declined matches remain visible in the "Declined" filter view

**Priority:** High

---

### User Story 4: Employer Views Matched Candidates

**As an** employer
**I want** to view matched candidates for each of my job postings
**So that** I can evaluate and act on AI recommendations

**Acceptance Criteria:**

- [ ] Employer selects a job posting to view its matches
- [ ] Candidates listed by confidence score (strongest first) by default
- [ ] Each match card shows: confidence badge, match summary, seeker status, employer status
- [ ] Employer can view the full match reasoning by expanding a card
- [ ] Match count and workflow status visible per posting

**Priority:** High

---

### User Story 5: Employer Accepts or Declines a Candidate

**As an** employer
**I want** to accept or decline a matched candidate
**So that** I can progress promising candidates toward contact exchange

**Acceptance Criteria:**

- [ ] Accept and Decline buttons visible only when the employer's status is Pending
- [ ] Confirmation prompt shown before declining
- [ ] After action, the match status updates immediately in the UI
- [ ] Once accepted or declined, the action cannot be reversed

**Priority:** High

---

### User Story 6: Mutual Accept Contact Reveal

**As an** employer who has accepted a candidate that also accepted
**I want** to see the job seeker's contact information
**So that** I can reach out to schedule an interview

**Acceptance Criteria:**

- [ ] Contact information revealed only when both seeker and employer have accepted
- [ ] Contact info displayed prominently on the match card with a visual distinction (e.g., highlighted section)
- [ ] Contact info includes at minimum: name and location (as provided by the seeker)
- [ ] Job seeker sees a notification that their contact info has been shared with the employer

**Priority:** High

---

### User Story 7: Email Notifications for Matches

**As a** user (seeker or employer)
**I want** to receive email notifications when new matches are created or when the other party accepts
**So that** I can take timely action

**Acceptance Criteria:**

- [ ] Email sent to job seeker when a new match is created for them
- [ ] Email sent to employer when a job seeker accepts their match
- [ ] Email sent to job seeker when an employer accepts their match (mutual accept achieved)
- [ ] Emails include a link back to the match in the dashboard
- [ ] Email sending is asynchronous and does not block the match creation or status update flow
- [ ] Users can opt out of match notification emails in their settings

**Priority:** Medium

---

## Functional Requirements

**FR-1:** The seeker match dashboard displays all matches associated with the authenticated seeker, with default sort by confidence score descending.

**FR-2:** The employer match dashboard displays matches grouped by job posting, with default sort by confidence score descending within each posting.

**FR-3:** Match status filters are available for both seekers and employers: All, Pending, Accepted, Declined.

**FR-4:** Sort options include "Best Match" (confidence score descending) and "Newest" (creation date descending).

**FR-5:** Accept and Decline actions are available only when the user's own status for that match is Pending.

**FR-6:** Declining a match requires a confirmation step; accepting does not.

**FR-7:** Match status updates are reflected in the UI immediately after the action succeeds, without a full page reload.

**FR-8:** When both parties have accepted a match (mutual accept), the employer sees the seeker's contact information on the match card.

**FR-9:** The seeker is informed when their contact information has been shared (mutual accept notification).

**FR-10:** Email notifications are sent asynchronously on: new match creation, and mutual accept.

**FR-11:** Notification preferences (opt-in/opt-out) are stored per user and respected by the notification system.

**FR-12:** Cursor-based pagination is used for match lists to support growing data sets.

---

## Non-Functional Requirements

**NFR-1 (Performance):** Match list pages load in under 1 second (90th percentile) for up to 100 matches.

**NFR-2 (Responsiveness):** Dashboard is usable on mobile viewports (min 375px width).

**NFR-3 (Accessibility):** Interactive elements (buttons, filters, sort controls) are keyboard-navigable and have appropriate ARIA labels.

**NFR-4 (Security):** Contact information is never included in API responses unless mutual accept has occurred. The server enforces this — client-side hiding is not sufficient.

**NFR-5 (Reliability):** Failed email delivery does not affect match creation or status update operations. Email failures are logged and retried.

---

## Edge Cases & Error Handling

1. **No matches yet:** Show empty state with explanation. Seeker: "Matches will appear when employers find you as a potential fit." Employer: "Activate a job posting to start the matching process."

2. **Race condition on mutual accept:** If both parties accept simultaneously, the contact info population must happen exactly once. The system handles this idempotently.

3. **Email delivery failure:** Logged and retried up to 3 times. Never blocks the core match flow.

4. **Stale UI state:** If a user has the dashboard open and the other party changes status, the user sees the stale state until they refresh or take an action. Eventual consistency is acceptable for MVP.

5. **Large match volumes:** Employer postings may generate hundreds of matches. Pagination (cursor-based, default 20 per page) prevents performance degradation.

6. **Declined match visibility:** Declined matches remain visible under the "Declined" filter. They cannot be un-declined.

7. **User with no postings (employer):** Dashboard shows prompt to create first posting.

8. **Contact info incomplete:** If the seeker has not provided location, the contact reveal shows only what is available with a note about incomplete data.

---

## Success Metrics

- Job seekers review at least 80% of their pending matches within 48 hours of notification
- Mutual accept rate is at least 15% of total matches surfaced
- Email notification delivery rate > 99%
- Dashboard page load < 1s at 90th percentile

---

## Out of Scope

- Real-time WebSocket updates (polling or action-based refresh is sufficient for MVP)
- In-app chat between matched parties
- Calendar integration for scheduling interviews
- Bulk accept/decline operations (deferred to Feature 17)
- SMS notifications
